topic: What system lets many concurrent AI coding sessions commit and push to one shared long-lived branch continuously, with zero operator-visible divergence blockers, an always-current IDE checkout, and no loss of concurrent uncommitted work?

reason: Detached fan-out lineage is restricted to its artifact directory.

focus: Git publication invariants and comparison of concurrent fast-forward integration strategies
noveltyJustification: All 6 findings are new in this initialized lineage; no prior iteration findings existed.
keyQuestions: Which integration strategy best guarantees continuous fast-forward publication under concurrent writers: rebase/retry, serialization, merge queue, or scratch-index/ref-level commit construction?
answeredQuestions: Which integration strategy best guarantees continuous fast-forward publication under concurrent writers: rebase/retry, serialization, merge queue, or scratch-index/ref-level commit construction?
ruledOut: Uncoordinated direct pushes as a zero-rejection guarantee
A hosted merge queue as the sole low-latency continuous autosync mechanism

focus: Workspace isolation and safe freshness for concurrent AI sessions and a dedicated IDE projection
noveltyJustification: All 6 findings add new workspace-isolation or refresh-safety evidence beyond iteration 1's publication strategy.
keyQuestions: Which workspace model and update protocol keep session work isolated while maintaining an always-current IDE view?
What invariants prevent freshness updates from disturbing concurrent uncommitted work?
answeredQuestions: Which workspace model and update protocol keep session work isolated while maintaining an always-current IDE view?
ruledOut: One shared working tree for concurrent AI sessions
Long-lived clone --shared or --reference alternates as the default isolation boundary
Watch-triggered rebase, autostash, or reset in active session worktrees

focus: Crash-safe publication invariants, reachability, remote acknowledgement, and forward-only rollback
noveltyJustification: Six findings are fully new and two partially extend prior isolation/autostash evidence, yielding (6 + 0.5×2) / 8 = 0.875.
keyQuestions: What invariants, failure recovery, and rollback prevent lost uncommitted work, orphaned autostashes, rejected pushes, and force-push loss?
answeredQuestions: What invariants, failure recovery, and rollback prevent lost uncommitted work, orphaned autostashes, rejected pushes, and force-push loss?
ruledOut: An object ID recorded only in an application journal as a garbage-collection reachability guarantee
A temporary autostash as the durability boundary for unattended session recovery
Backward ref movement or force-push as rollback of an already published shared-branch commit

focus: Automation authority, checkout-free conflict preflight, partitioning, and semantic-conflict quarantine
noveltyJustification: Six findings are fully new and one partially extends the prior orchestration treatment of unavoidable conflicts, yielding (6 + 0.5×1) / 7 = 0.929.
keyQuestions: Which automation surface and conflict-avoidance rules are reliable enough for unattended AI sessions?
answeredQuestions: Which automation surface and conflict-avoidance rules are reliable enough for unattended AI sessions?
ruledOut: Blind FIFO bypass without proven path and dependency independence

focus: Existing-art comparison, adversarial architecture synthesis, exact semantic-conflict behavior, and acceptance tests
noveltyJustification: Five findings are fully new and two partially synthesize prior evidence; (5 + 0.5×2) / 7 + 0.10 simplicity bonus = 0.957.
keyQuestions: What does existing art prove, and what default architecture, trade-offs, and testable acceptance conditions follow?
answeredQuestions: What does existing art prove, and what default architecture, trade-offs, and testable acceptance conditions follow?
ruledOut: Using git-auto-sync or Git Town as the multi-session publication authority
Treating filesystem conflict copies as Git semantic integration
Using GitHub merge queue or Gerrit alone as the end-to-end local and remote solution
Claiming arbitrary overlapping edits can always be auto-merged
